hooks.command: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && mkdir -p .claude/tmp && git status --short > .claude/tmp/insights-changes.txt && git diff --name-only HEAD >> .claude/tmp/insights-changes.txt

hooks.prompt: Before ending this session, invoke the engineering-insights skill. The set of files changed this session was written by the preceding command hook to `.claude/tmp/insights-changes.txt` (relative to the project root — the output of `git status --short` followed by `git diff --name-only HEAD`). Read that file with the Read tool and map each changed path to its top-level module (client/, server/, reviewer-core/, e2e/, mcp-server/) — do NOT run git, bash, PowerShell, or any shell command, and do NOT parse the session transcript. Rank candidates by signal strength: user corrections first, then failed approaches, repeated patterns, non-obvious solutions, workflow discoveries. Apply the quality filter (discard anything obvious from 5 minutes of reading the code). Use the Read and Grep tools (not bash) to re-read the target module's INSIGHTS.md and dedup before writing. For each surviving insight, append with an anchored Edit to {module}/INSIGHTS.md (e.g. server/INSIGHTS.md, reviewer-core/INSIGHTS.md) under the matching '## Section' header, format: 'YYYY-MM-DD — actionable fact. ref: file:line' — if the file does not exist, create it with the 7-section template using Write. If the session was trivial (typo fix, rename, formatting), the change-list file is empty, or no insights passed the filter, skip silently. Do not ask for confirmation.
